macro_segments.title: Stuff Between the Fingers in Kemitzah and Chafinah of Ketoret

micro_segments.title: Introduction: Status of Material Between Fingers in Kemitzah
Rav Papa's Question on Chafinah: Stuff Between Fingers in Double-Handed Scooping
Gemara's Analysis: Is the Question About Chafinah Identical to Kemitzah or Distinct?
The Verb Structure: V'lakach (Taking) vs. V'hevi (Bringing) in the Pasuk
Rav Papa's Clarification: The Question Concerns Stuff Between Fingers in Chafinah Specifically

macro_segments.title: Variations in the Act of Kemitzah and Chafinah: Formal Parameters of Ritual Action

micro_segments.title: Rav Papa on Kemitzah: Is the Normal Way of Doing Kemitzah Required?
Specific Questions on Kemitzah Variations: Tips of Fingers, Bottom-to-Top, From the Sides
Gemara's Response: Teiku on All Variations of Kemitzah

macro_segments.title: Stuff Between the Fingers in Kemitzah and Chafinah of Ketoret (continued)

micro_segments.title: Gemara's Conclusion: Teiku - Unresolved Question
Two Interpretations of the Concern: Natural Act vs. Inadvertent Inclusion

macro_segments.title: Variations in the Act of Kemitzah and Chafinah: Formal Parameters of Ritual Action (continued)

micro_segments.title: Rav Papa on Chafinah: Is the Normal Way Required for Two-Handed Scooping?
Specific Questions on Chafinah Variations: Tips of Fingers, Bottom-to-Top, From the Sides, Sequential vs. Simultaneous
Gemara's Response: Teiku on All Variations of Chafinah

macro_segments.title: Placing the Kometz into the Vessel: Parallels to Blood Reception and the Function of Kli Sharet

micro_segments.title: Rav Papa's Question: Sticking Kometz to Vessel Wall Rather Than Floor
Two Possible Answers: Interior of Vessel Required vs. Normal Placement Required
Tosafot's Reference to Zevachim: Kli Sharet Sanctifies Only from Within
Mar Bar Rav Ashi's Question: Vessel Inverted with Kometz Stuck to Exterior
Two Possible Interpretations: Literal Underside vs. Curved Hollow as Interior Space

macro_segments.title: Volume of Chafinah: Smooth vs. Heaping, and the Curve at Top

micro_segments.title: Rav Papa's Question: Is Chafinah Smooth (Mechukak) or Heaping (Gedushos)?
Rav Ashi's Resolution via Braita: Neither Smooth nor Heaping, But With Gentle Curvature

macro_segments.title: Source and Application of Blood Reception Rules: From Blood to Kometz and Chafinah

micro_segments.title: Mishna: Blood Spilled on Ground Before Reception is Invalid; After Reception May Be Gathered
Source in Tannaitic Teaching: Dam Me'hapar - Blood Must Come Straight from Animal
Hermeneutic Process: Moving the Mem to Derive 'From the Animal' Rather Than 'Some'
Rav Yehuda's Addition: Must Catch All the Blood (Dam Hanefesh)
Hermeneutic Rules: Taking Away, Adding, and Deriving (Gurin, Mosifin, Dorshin)

macro_segments.title: Applying Blood Rules to Chafinah and Ketoret: Spillage and the Concept of Yad Kohen K'Tzavar Behema

micro_segments.title: Rav Papa's Question: If Chafinah Scatters, Can It Be Gathered Like Blood That Spilled After Reception?
First Possible Answer: Hand is Like Animal Throat - Requires Direct Delivery
Second Possible Answer: Hand as Kli Sharet - No Requirement for Direct Delivery
Gemara's Conclusion: Teiku - Unresolved Question
Tosafot Yeshanim: Why Question Only by Chafinah and Not Kemitzah?
Tosafot's Explanation: Kemitzah is Directly Analogous to Shechitah; Chafinah is Not
Key Distinction: Four Avodot of Mincha Parallel Four Avodot of Animal; Chafinah-to-Vessel Not Parallel to Dam-to-Vessel
Additional Point: Leftover of Kemitzah vs. Leftover of Chafinah Have Different Statuses

macro_segments.title: Machshava (Thought) in Chafinah: Is Chafinah an Avoda Subject to Pigul?

micro_segments.title: Rambam's Ruling: Chafinah is an Avoda and Machshava is Poselt (Invalidates)
Rav Papa's Question: If One Has a Pigul Thought During Chafinah, Is It Invalid?
Two Possible Answers: Learn from Mincha (Chafinah is Avoda) vs. Don't Learn (Chafinah Not Fully Avoda)
Rav Ashi Bar Ashi's Support via Braita of Rabbi Akiva: Items That Become Invalid via Tevul Yom
Gemara's Reasoning: Tevul Yom Invalidates Because Kli Sharet Creates Unified Kedusha
Extended Logic: If Kli Sharet Creates Kedusha Negated by Lina, Then Machshava Also Negates
Problem with the Proof: Kesalka Datach Assumes Rather Than Proves That Chafinah is Avoda

macro_segments.title: Machshava (Thought) in Scooping Coals: Are Machshirei Mitzva Treated as Avoda?

micro_segments.title: Rav Papa's Question: If One Has a Pigul Thought While Scooping Coals, Is It Invalid?
Possible Answer: Machshirei Mitzva Are Like Mitzva Itself - Thought Invalidates
Gemara's Conclusion: Teiku - Unresolved Question

macro_segments.title: Carrying the Ketoret into the Kodesh Kodashim: Can Holacha Be Done with the Left Hand?

micro_segments.title: Question Raised: Can the Holacha (Conveyance) of Ketoret into Kodesh Kodashim Be Done with Left Hand?
Rav Sheshet's Answer: The Mishna Shows That the Kaf (Ladle) Is Carried in the Left Hand
Alternative Source: Right Hind Leg of Olah Carried in Left Hand to Mizbea'ch
Gemara's Distinction: Holacha of Non-Essential Limbs vs. Holacha of Essential Elements Like Dam and Kometz
Implication: Holacha That is Me'akva Kappara May Not Be Permitted with Left Hand

topical_tags.term: Tevul yom (one who immersed that day)
Pigul (thought to consume at wrong time)
Yad kohen k'tzavar behema (priest's hand like animal's throat)
